steps.title: Identify the core disagreement between the texts
steps.content: Text 1 says Kolvereid and Moen "convincingly demonstrated" their findings — treating the evidence as persuasive. Text 2 says the study only collected post-training data, making it "unclear whether the training actually had an effect" — treating the evidence as methodologically flawed and unpersuasive. The disagreement is about whether the study's evidence is convincing.

steps.title: Eliminate each choice
steps.content:
• Choice A — Is the cost of conducting entrepreneurial training programs typically offset by the programs' benefits?: Neither text discusses cost; this topic is absent from both. ✗
  • Choice B — Did Kolvereid and Moen's study provide persuasive evidence that its results are attributable to the entrepreneurial training participants received?: Text 1 says yes (convincingly demonstrated); Text 2 says no (unclear causation due to missing pre-training data). Direct disagreement. ✓
  • Choice C — Did Kolvereid and Moen report that participants who received the entrepreneurial training showed strong intentions to become entrepreneurs?: Both texts confirm this fact — it is a point of agreement, not disagreement. ✗
  • Choice D — Are there convincing experimental results showing that different types of entrepreneurial training programs are associated with different outcomes?: Neither text compares different types of programs; this is outside the scope of both. ✗
That confirms B is correct.
